source_fetch.method: read-only raw.githubusercontent.com fetch at pinned revision
source_fetch.note: Upstream source bodies were inspected for review and not vendored into this repository.

candidate_specific_findings: The upstream Skill contains useful spec-first phase gates and acceptance discipline.
It includes example commands such as build, test, lint, and dev commands; those must remain project-specific examples, not executable defaults.

It references upstream incremental-implementation, test-driven-development, and context-engineering Skills; these references must be remapped to existing curated Skills, recipes, native reasoning, or rejected during adaptation.
It requires human review and approval before implementation, which aligns with the MVP human-gate model.
must_neutralize: any wording that implies implementation may proceed without the active repository's authority
mvp02_remaining_work: decide whether the useful material belongs in an existing Skill, a recipe, or rejection evidence
produce adapted text only after human approval
record overlap and routing effect before any release change

candidate_specific_findings: The upstream Skill adds useful ADR lifecycle and documentation-authority guidance.
It uses concrete examples such as PostgreSQL, Prisma, npm, OpenAPI, and TypeScript; those must be treated as illustrative examples, not universal defaults.
It references CLAUDE.md and rules files as agent-facing documentation; this must be neutralized to generic agent instruction files or repository-local guidance.
It distinguishes historical ADRs from current decisions, which supports artifact hygiene and continuity.
must_neutralize: any claim that every project must use the same documentation structure
mvp02_remaining_work: decide which ADR lifecycle rules should become generic curated guidance
separate repository-local documentation standards from Skill behavior
produce adapted text only after human approval

candidate_specific_findings: The upstream Skill provides useful multi-axis review guidance across correctness, readability, architecture, security, and performance.
It contains approval language for PR/change review; curated adaptation must keep final merge/release authority with the active repository and human owner.
It references security-and-hardening and performance guidance; those references must be mapped to existing curated Skills, official security capabilities, or recipes.
It includes npm audit as an example dependency check; this must become ecosystem-specific guidance rather than a universal command.
must_neutralize: any wording that makes review mandatory for trivial or low-risk tasks
mvp02_remaining_work: decide whether review-quality guidance is a merge into review, a recipe, or both
ensure security review remains owned by the appropriate security capability
produce adapted text only after human approval

reviews.remaining_gate: Human approval is still required before adaptation, approval, manifest changes, routing projection changes, or runtime installation.
